title: Agent design dashboard
subtitle: Rendered from the structured design package
overall_verdict: The package shows an implementation-ready design with a clear control/mechanism split. The main remaining work is operational hardening and regression coverage.

overview_cards.title: System shape
overview_cards.description: The business boundary is narrow enough to avoid premature decomposition.

overview_cards.title: Readiness
overview_cards.description: Contracts and runtime are mostly defined, while ops and eval still need implementation.

overview_cards.title: Critical risk
overview_cards.description: The design can ship only after degraded-path and schema-drift signals are instrumented.

architecture.heading: Solution map
architecture.text: The agent normalizes requests into typed catalog queries and hands the payload to the search API.
architecture.mermaid:
flowchart LR
    U["User request"] --> A["Catalog query planner"] --> S["Catalog search API"]

critical_risks: Schema drift can invalidate the generated query contract.
Timeout handling is designed but not yet instrumented in production.
Regression coverage must include unsupported-intent and degraded-path cases.

cells.note: Catalog search API is the primary consumer.
Input shape is fixed in the design package.
Success and empty paths are specified.
Degraded contract is explicit and retry-aware.

implementation_phases.title: Phase 1. Contracts
implementation_phases.description: Freeze consumer-facing schemas and parser expectations.

implementation_phases.title: Phase 2. Runtime
implementation_phases.description: Implement schema lookup, validation, and bounded retries.

implementation_phases.title: Phase 3. Eval and ops
implementation_phases.description: Add scenario regressions, tracing, and alerting.

agents_lead: The cards below should be enough to start implementation without going back to a prose-only design document.
